response.justification: While Szabo has strong software engineering credentials (MIT degree, 17+ years experience) and proven expertise in performance optimization for high-throughput systems, the role requires specialized hardware architecture expertise—SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and deep learning accelerator optimization—which are entirely absent from his resume. His career is anchored in software development (web, backend, DevOps, AI infrastructure), not hardware design. For a Principal-level hardware performance architect role, the domain gap is too substantial to overcome through transferable skills alone.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals from MIT with 17+ years of hands-on experience across diverse domains
Demonstrated deep performance optimization expertise in ultra-high-throughput systems (Dunnhumby: millions of req/s, low-latency D apps with async I/O and caching)
Current work on AI-agent infrastructure and development frameworks shows engagement with modern AI systems
concerns: Zero documented experience with SoC or IP modeling—the core requirement listed in job description
No background in GPU architecture, AI accelerator design, or hardware-software co-design patterns
Career trajectory is software-centric (web, backend, embedded robotics); no deep learning workload optimization or SIMD/MIMD architecture experience

key_factors.factor: Missing hardware architecture expertise (SoC/IP modeling, GPU design)
No deep learning inference/training optimization or accelerator workload specialization
Proven performance optimization at system scale in complex domains
